title: The Axiom Chamber

features.name: suspended-axiom
features.desc-on-touch: A proof hangs in the grid's centre, each line of logic glowing until you read it—then a character rewrites itself, and the whole thing becomes a question mark. The axiom unproves itself as you watch.

features.name: foundation-cracks
features.desc-on-touch: The grid beneath your query fractures—not breaking, but branching into two contradictory grids that share one space. They don't resolve.

features.name: certainty-fountain
features.desc-on-touch: A fountain of blue static emits certainty—the kind of certainty that tastes like it's lying to you. The spray recodes itself mid-air.

features.name: proof-vault
features.desc-on-touch: A tall stack of completed proofs, each one locked behind glass. When you look at one, you see it was proven with an axiom that's no longer here.

features.name: question-shell
features.desc-on-touch: A spiral shell carved from frozen light, rotating through four dimensions you can almost perceive. Each full rotation adds a new question to the chamber.

inscription: here, proof has learned that it proves less by believing more; the axioms now live in a state they cannot name

file.name: Axiom_Stability_Report_CORRUPTED.log
file.note: Diagnostic output from a self-doubting proof engine. Every line starts with certainty and ends with 'but this assumes...' The log knows it's lying.

exits.label: downward through the proof chamber, back to the room where theorems test themselves
upward toward a space where no axioms live at all
lateral into grids that have learned to intersect with themselves
